name: nitrogen_vent_system_867bf8cc
description: Design a simple gaseous nitrogen venting system consisting of a nitrogen gas source node connected to ambient through a single orifice. The nitrogen node contains gaseous nitrogen at elevated pressure and vents to ambient conditions (atmospheric pressure ~101325 Pa) through the orifice. The system should establish steady flow from the nitrogen node to ambient. FIXED: nitrogen node initial pressure (elevated above ambient), ambient pressure (101325 Pa), single orifice connection. TUNABLE: nitrogen node initial pressure value, orifice size/flow characteristics, nitrogen node volume and initial mass.

notes: Nitrogen node should start at elevated pressure above ambient to drive flow
Ambient node represents atmospheric conditions at ~101325 Pa
Single orifice connection between nitrogen_node and ambient
Flow direction should be from nitrogen_node to ambient

checks.description: System establishes non-zero mass flow through the orifice
No nonphysical or degenerate warnings during simulation
Ambient node pressure remains near atmospheric pressure (101325 Pa +/- 100 Pa)
Ambient node pressure remains near atmospheric pressure (101325 Pa +/- 100 Pa)
Nitrogen node starts at elevated pressure above ambient (at least 200 kPa above atmospheric)
Orifice connection has positive mass flow from nitrogen to ambient
Nitrogen node mass decreases as it vents to ambient
